Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission
Off Calendar May-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert A. Padway for Plaintiff and moving party Ila Loeb and moving party
Catherine M. Lee for Defendant and opposint party Washington Mutual Bank, FA

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: APPEARANCES:
On calendar Monday, May 15, 2006. Plaintiff Ila Loeb's motion to compel further responses to requests for admission is taken off calendar per stipulation of parties. Comm. Bruce E. Chan.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar May-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert A. Padway for Plaintiff and moving party Ila Loeb and moving party
Catherine M. Lee for Defendant and opposint party Washington Mutual Bank, FA

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
On calendar Monday, May 15, 2006. Plaintiff Ila Loeb's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents 25 -27 (set #3) is Denied.
The objection that the requests seek information that is irrelevant to the subject matter of the action and are not likely to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence is sustained. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Comm. Bruce E. Chan.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories; Separate Statement
Off Calendar May-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert A. Padway for Plaintiff and moving party Ila Loeb and moving party
Catherine M. Lee for Defendant and opposint party Washington Mutual Bank, FA

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
On calendar Monday, May 15, 2006. Plaintiff Ila Loeb's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories 12 and 13 (set #3) is Denied.
The objection that the questions seek information that is irrelevant to the subject matter of the action and are not likely to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence is sustained. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Comm. Bruce E. Chan.

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Feb-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
ROBERT A. PADWAY, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY
CATHERINE M. LEE, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY

MOTION ARGUED. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING.

RULING: Plaintiff's motions to compel further responses to request for production of documents: Granted in part and denied in part.
14 --- The request is modified to only require production of the final contents of advertisements relating to home and construction loans that were published in California from August 30, 2001 to date.
15, 21 --- Granted
16 - 20, 22 --- Denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Feb-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
ROBERT A. PADWAY, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY
CATHERINE M. LEE, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY

MOTION ARGUED. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. RULING: Plaintiff's motions to compel further responses to special interrogatories: The motion is denied as moot as supplemental responses were served after the filing of the motion. The reservation of the right to amend the answer is stricken. The response must be full and complete at the time it is made. If there is a need to amend, it must be made in compliance with CCP Section 2030.310. (610/CHAN)
